{{Quote|Antom in the Station Master's Office would be thrilled to see you. I'm sure he's gotten himself into some kind of sticky situation that requires diplomacy.|[[Han Solo/Legends|Han Solo]], to a spacer|<ref name="SWG:YNW">{{SWGcite|exp=NGE|type=general|You Need Work|Tansarii Point Station}}</ref>}}
'''Antom Moria''' was a [[Gender/Legends|male]] [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Officer/Legends|officer]] who was living during the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]].

==Biography==
{{Quote|I was hoping that you would come and see me. I know that you are probably busy but we have a situation that we could really use your help with.|Antom Moria, to a spacer|<ref name="SWG:OA" />}}
After the [[Battle of Yavin/Legends|Battle of Yavin]], Antom Moria resided on board [[Tansarii Point Station/Legends|Tansarii Point Station]], in the [[Bright Jewel system/Legends|Bright Jewel system]].<ref name="SWG:OA" /> During that time, he was training a young student named [[Neina]] to become an officer.<ref name="SWG:AMS">{{SWGcite|exp=NGE|type=class|Officer|Antom's Missing Student|Tansarii Point Station}}</ref>

In [[1 ABY/Legends|1 ABY]],<ref name="SWG_timeline">{{SWG_timeline}}</ref> Antom Moria was very concerned by the escalating conflict between the [[Car'das/Legends|Car'das]] controlling Tansarii Point Station and the [[Rak'qua]] living in neighboring [[Station Gamma]].<ref name="SWG:OA" /> The officer thus dispatched his student Neina to Station Gamma in order to discuss with a Rak'qua Fervent, one of their leaders, but the Rak'qua refused to discuss with the young woman and captured her.<ref name="SWG:AMS" /> Two days later, a spacer who was also an officer under training arrived on Tansarii Point Station, along with [[Han Solo/Legends|Han Solo]].<ref name="SWG:OA" /> The spacer agreed with helping Antom and soon departed to Station Gamma to negotiate the release of Antom's missing student. The Rak'qua Fervent was not willing to free his hostage but proposed a test of honor: if the spacer could hunt down and defeat a Rak'qua [[Shaman/Legends|shaman]] in a fair fight, the student would be released. Since the spacer succeeded, to the Fervent great surprise, Neina was set free and returned safe to Antom Moria.<ref name="SWG:AMS" />

Antom Moria then continued to investigate the reasons of the war between the Car'das and the Rak'qua.<ref name="SWG:PaW">{{SWGcite|exp=NGE|type=class|Officer|Preventing a War|Tansarii Point Station}}</ref> He finally obtained evidences from the [[Bothan/Legends|Bothan]] [[Spy/Legends|spy]] [[Lercer Gaia]] that a local arms dealer was involved in the outbreak of the conflict.<ref name="SWG:PaW" /><ref name="SWG:TSG">{{SWGcite|exp=NGE|type=class|Spy|The Spy's Game|Tansarii Point Station}}</ref> The officer thus tasked the spacer to travel again to Station Gamma and present the evidences to the Rak'qua Fervent. Hence, Moria was able to convince both parties that the war was meaningless and managed to prevent escalation of the conflict.<ref name="SWG:PaW" />

After those events, the spacer left Tansarii Point Station and traveled to [[Tatooine/Legends|Tatooine]] and [[Naboo/Legends|Naboo]]. There, Antom Moria introduced the spacer to the officers [[Ellis Arrison]] and [[Eom Nia]] who undertook the training of the spacer.<ref name="SWG:OM1">{{SWGcite|exp=NGE|type=class|Officer|Officer Mission 1|Tatooine}}</ref><ref name="SWG:OM2">{{SWGcite|exp=NGE|type=class|Officer|Officer Mission 2|Naboo}}</ref>

==Behind the scenes==
Antom Moria appeared as a [[Wikipedia:Non-player character|non-player character]] in the [[2003]] [[Star Wars video games|video game]] ''[[Star Wars Galaxies]]'', a [[Wikipedia:Massively multiplayer online role-playing game|massively multiplayer online roleplaying game]] developed by [[Sony]] Online Entertainment and published by [[LucasArts]],<ref name="SWG:OA" /> prior to its closure on [[December 15]], 2011.<ref name="Shutdown">{{SonyCite|url=playerinfo|text=IMPORTANT INFORMATION ABOUT ''STAR WARS'' GALAXIES™}}</ref> Antom Moria was added to the game as part of the "[[W:c:swg:New Player Tutorial|New Player Tutorial]]," introduced with the release of the "[[W:c:swg:Publish 25 Notes for November 15, 2005|Publish 25]]," a.k.a. the "[[W:c:swg:New Game Enhancements|New Game Enhancements]]," on [[November 15]], [[2005]].<ref name="SonyCite-Publish Archive 2005">{{SonyCite|url=players/content.vm?id=64886&resource=publish|text=Publish Archive 2005}}</ref>
